name: fix-multi-tenant-infrastructure-gaps
title: fix: Multi-tenant infrastructure gaps in Headless Framework
description: Fix missing multi-tenant glue in Headless Framework: default ICurrentTenant registration, claim-based tenant resolution middleware, DI helper, and documentation
verification_command: dotnet test --project tests/Headless.Api.Tests.Unit/Headless.Api.Tests.Unit.csproj -- --filter-class Tests.Setup.ApiSetupTests && dotnet test --project tests/Headless.Api.Tests.Integration/Headless.Api.Tests.Integration.csproj -- --filter-class Tests.TenantResolutionMiddlewareTests

userStories.title: Fix default ICurrentTenant registration [XS]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write test that resolves ICurrentTenant from DI and asserts it is CurrentTenant (not NullCurrentTenant) → change Setup.cs registration → verify PASS.

Change `TryAddSingleton<ICurrentTenant, NullCurrentTenant>()` to `TryAddSingleton<ICurrentTenant, CurrentTenant>()` in `src/Headless.Api/Setup.cs`. CurrentTenant reads from AsyncLocalCurrentTenantAccessor which defaults to null — same behavior as NullCurrentTenant when no tenant is set.
acceptanceCriteria: ICurrentTenant resolves to CurrentTenant (not NullCurrentTenant) after AddHeadlessApi()
When no tenant is set, ICurrentTenant.Id returns null and IsAvailable returns false (backward compatible)
notes: This unblocks the existing ScopedCache<PermissionGrantCacheItem> registration which depends on ICurrentTenant.Id being non-null when a tenant IS set

userStories.title: Add claim-based tenant resolution middleware [S]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write integration test (authenticated request with tenant_id claim → ICurrentTenant.Id returns expected value; unauthenticated → null; missing claim → null) → implement TenantResolutionMiddleware → add UseHeadlessTenantResolution() extension → verify PASS.

Middleware reads tenant_id from ClaimsPrincipal via GetTenantId() extension and calls ICurrentTenant.Change() with a using scope. Must run after UseAuthentication() and before UseAuthorization().

Key design decision: when GetTenantId() returns null, skip Change() entirely (do NOT call Change(null)). This preserves the distinction between 'never set' (Current == null) and 'explicitly host' (Current.TenantId == null).
acceptanceCriteria: UseHeadlessTenantResolution() extension method available on IApplicationBuilder
Authenticated request with tenant_id claim sets ICurrentTenant.Id for the request scope
Unauthenticated requests and requests missing tenant_id claim proceed with null tenant (skip Change(), no error)
Tenant scope is properly disposed after request completes (no bleed to next request)
Middleware reads claim type from MultiTenancyOptions if registered (configurable via US-003)
notes: Consider making the claim type configurable via MultiTenancyOptions from US-003

userStories.title: Add AddHeadlessMultiTenancy() DI helper [S]
userStories.description:
TDD order: write test that AddHeadlessMultiTenancy() registers CurrentTenant and accepts custom claim type → implement extension method on IHostApplicationBuilder → verify PASS.

Provide a single opt-in method that ensures CurrentTenant is registered (overriding NullCurrentTenant if needed via AddOrReplace) and stores MultiTenancyOptions for the middleware to consume.
acceptanceCriteria: AddHeadlessMultiTenancy() available as extension on IHostApplicationBuilder
Calling it ensures ICurrentTenant resolves to CurrentTenant even if AddHeadlessApi() already registered NullCurrentTenant
Optional Action<MultiTenancyOptions> parameter allows configuring custom claim type (default: tenant_id)

userStories.title: Add multi-tenancy documentation [S]
userStories.description:
Sequential: write docs/llms/multi-tenancy.txt → update llms.txt index → verify content covers all scenarios.

Create comprehensive documentation covering: DI setup with AddHeadlessMultiTenancy(), middleware registration and ordering (after UseAuthentication, before UseAuthorization), IMultiTenant entity configuration, EF global query filters, permission cache integration via ScopedCache, background job tenant iteration pattern (foreach tenant → using Change()), message consumer header-based pattern, SignalR limitations, and three-state tenant semantics (never set vs explicitly null vs tenant ID).
acceptanceCriteria: docs/llms/multi-tenancy.txt exists with end-to-end setup guide
llms.txt index updated to include multi-tenancy.txt
Documentation covers: DI registration, middleware ordering, entity setup, permissions, caching, non-HTTP contexts (background jobs + message consumers), and three-state tenant semantics

findings: Setup.cs registers NullCurrentTenant as default — confirmed still present
Permissions.Core/Setup.cs already registers ScopedCache<PermissionGrantCacheItem> with tenant scope provider (() => $"t:{ICurrentTenant.Id}") — cache issue #4 already fixed
ScopedCache only works when ICurrentTenant resolves to CurrentTenant (not NullCurrentTenant) — fixing #1 unblocks #4
zad-ngo has no tenant resolution code — AccountClaimsPrincipalGenerator doesn't emit tenant_id claim yet (FOUND-02 not started)
EF global query filters for IMultiTenant already work via HeadlessEntityModelProcessor — just needs ICurrentTenant.Id to be properly set

projectLearnings: User approved narrowing verification from repo-wide dotnet test to focused Headless.Api unit and integration suites for this change
